description: independent U.S. government agency
description_wiki: The Federal Communications Commission (FCC) is an agency of the United States federal government that regulates communications by radio, television, wire, internet, Wi-Fi, satellite, and cable across the United States.

description: former American independent agency
description_wiki: The Reconstruction Finance Corporation (RFC) was an independent agency of the United States federal government that served as a lender of last resort to US banks and businesses.

description: 1939–1945 global conflict
description_wiki: World War II, or the Second World War (1 September 1939 – 2 September 1945), was a global conflict between two coalitions: the Allies and the Axis powers.

president of the United States from 1933 to 1945 (1882–1945)
description_wiki: Franklin Delano Roosevelt (January 30, 1882 – April 12, 1945), also known as FDR, was the 32nd president of the United States, serving from 1933 until his death in 1945.

description: 1941 Japanese surprise attack on the U.S
description_wiki: On December 7, 1941, the Empire of Japan launched a surprise military strike on the United States Pacific Fleet at its naval base at Pearl Harbor on Oahu, Hawaii.

description: American politician
description_wiki: Frederick I. Thompson (September 29, 1875 – February 20, 1952) was an American businessman who served as a Commissioner of the Federal Communications Commission from 1939 to 1941.

description: American businessman (1893–1987)
description_wiki: Emil Schram (November 23, 1893 – September 18, 1987) was the president of the New York Stock Exchange from 1941 to 1951.

description: rail system in the United States of America
description_wiki: The Baltimore and Ohio Railroad (reporting marks BO, B&O) was the oldest railroad in the United States and the first steam-operated common carrier.

description: American stock exchange
description_wiki: The New York Stock Exchange (NYSE, nicknamed "the Big Board") is an American stock exchange headquartered at the New York Stock Exchange Building in the Financial District of Lower Manhattan in New York City.

description: president of the United States from 1961 to 1963 (1917–1963)
description_wiki: John Fitzgerald Kennedy (May 29, 1917 – November 22, 1963), also known as JFK, was the 35th president of the United States, serving from 1961 until his assassination in 1963.
The Fund for the Republic (1951–1959) was an organization created by the Ford Foundation and dedicated to protecting freedom of speech and other civil liberties in the United States.

description_wiki: The Ford Foundation is an American private foundation with the stated goal of advancing human welfare.

description: private university in Chicago, Illinois
description_wiki: The University of Chicago (UChicago, Chicago, UChi, or U of C) is a private research university in the Hyde Park community area of Chicago, Illinois, United States.

description_wiki: Princeton University is a private Ivy League research university in Princeton, New Jersey, United States.

description: president of the United States from 1963 to 1969 (1908–1973)
description_wiki: Lyndon Baines Johnson (August 27, 1908 – January 22, 1973), also known as LBJ, was the 36th president of the United States from 1963 until 1969.

description: U.S. Navy federal service academy in the Annapolis, Maryland area
description_wiki: The United States Naval Academy (USNA, Navy, or Annapolis) is a federal service academy adjacent to Annapolis, Maryland.

description: United States federal executive department
description_wiki: The United States Department of Justice (DOJ) is an executive department of the United States federal government that oversees the domestic enforcement of federal laws and the administration of justice.

description: federally-owned electric utility corporation in the United States
description_wiki: The Tennessee Valley Authority (TVA) is a federally-owned electric utility corporation in the United States.

description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: Oklahoma (  OH-klə-HOH-mə; Choctaw: Oklahumma, pronounced [oklahómma]) is a landlocked state in the South Central and Southwestern region of the United States.

description: Chief Justice of the Supreme Court of the United States from 1953 to 1969 (1891–1974)
description_wiki: Earl Warren (March 19, 1891 – July 9, 1974) was an American attorney and politician who served as the 30th governor of California from 1943 to 1953, and as the 14th chief justice of the United States from 1953 to 1969.

description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: California () is a state in the Western United States that lies on the Pacific Coast.

description_wiki: Albert Wayne Coy  (November 23, 1903 – September 24, 1957) served as chairman of the Federal Communications Commission from December 29, 1947, to February 21, 1952.

description: daily broadsheet newspaper in Washington, D.C.
description_wiki: The Washington Post (locally known as The Post and, informally, WaPo or WP) is an American daily newspaper published in Washington, D.C. It is the most widely circulated newspaper in the Washington metropolitan area and is considered a newspaper of record in the United States.

description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: Indiana (  IN-dee-AN-ə) is a state in the Midwestern region of the United States.
